It is a fantastic opportunity to work together to create positive change for individuals with disabilities and their families and carers.

records.specific_details: Emails sent to discuss the general importance of physics especially in terms of of education, skills and training and to start a conversation in that regard
records.intended_results: To raise awareness and profile of the Institute of Physics in general and to have active channels where we can go to discuss any upcoming issues that may arise among the parliamentary parties
lobbying_activities: Emailed a selection of TD's across parties representing Dublin Bay South and Dublin Bay North in order to start a process of contact from the Institute of Physics, the aim being to have active parliamentary channels with a view particularly to discussing areas related to Education, Training and Skills. We would like to contact these representatives to start with and we may need to contact any relevant representatives in the future as any physics related issues may arise. - Email

records.specific_details: Outreach to relevant stakeholders to introduce Liquid Gas Ireland’s newly appointed Senior Director for Government Affairs and Policy, Alan Farrell, and highlight LGI’s policy priorities for rural consumers and businesses.
records.intended_results: To brief political representatives on LGI’s policy priorities to support the decarbonisation of rural homes and businesses beyond the natural gas grid, emphasising the need for a ‘Just Transition’ in Ireland and the potential impact of the upcoming Renewable Heat Obligation Bill.

records.specific_details: Email concerning the E-liquids Products Tax and concerns around the mechanism chosen to implement the tax via self-declaration rather than as a tax-stamp. Hale also referred to research commissioned from KPMG which raised several important policy considerations around the E-Liquids products tax including the potential for a surge in illicit and grey market sales.
records.intended_results: A review of the proposal to apply an E-liquids tax using self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp.

records.specific_details: Support for Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug
records.intended_results: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. As such, Hale have never stocked HHC products, which Hale view as unregulated and potentially harmful substance.

dpo_entries.public_body: Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas
Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas

lobbying_activities: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale expressed strong support for the proposed legislative action to classify hexahydrocannabinol (HHC) as a Schedule 1 controlled drug under the Misuse of Drugs Act 1977. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. - Email

records.specific_details: Mental Health Bill 2024 and concerns re inability to provide treatment to all who will be involuntarily hospitalised due to current iteration.To provide descriptions using video recordings of people who had benefited from treatment under the current Mental Health Act explaining their illnesses and the necessary treatment provided in a timely manner.
records.intended_results: To provide an understanding (for TDs) of being severely unwell with psychosis and severe depression from people with lived experience and how that manifested for them. To give lived experience insight into how necessary, and importantly helpful to recovery, it was to receive timely and urgent treatment as a patient under the current Mental Health Act and whom were not at immediate risk of harm or harming.

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Housing, Local Government and Heritage

lobbying_activities: Email to all TD.s re with videos of people with lived experience of mental illness and involuntary hospitalisation. They each describe the debilitating effects of their illness and the benefits to their recovery and life of being treated under the Mental Health Act. - Email

records.specific_details: Programme for Government & Youth Policy
records.intended_results: Seeking inclusion of NYCI manifesto asks in Programme for Government on Youth structures, youth policy and youth work funding::Meetings to discuss the Programme for Government and youth policy in the new Oireachtas::Introduce NYCI and youth work to new TDs, Senators and Education and Youth Spokespeople::Establishment of an Oireachtas Committee on Youth in the new Oireachtas::Parliamentary Questions to be raised on issues related to the Ministerial brief as they related to youth affairs::Youth work services raised in Dáil debate on Programme for Government::To secure a meeting with the new Minister for Education and Youth
lobbying_activities: Email to all FF TDs to include asks in PFG - Email
Emails on the need for an Oireachtas Committee on Youth with briefing document - Email
Letter to Minister McEntee - Letter
Meetings with new Oireachtas members in Leinster House to introduce them to the work of NYCI and to youth work - Meeting
Zoom meeting with Pearse Doherty TD on youth work and youth policy - Virtual Meeting

records.specific_details: Uplift members sent emails to their Sinn Fèin TDs asking them to ensure that Sinn Féin is a party that is welcoming, inclusive and who listens to the experiences and expertise of transgender people.
records.intended_results: that Sinn Féin is a party that is welcoming, inclusive and who listens to the experiences and expertise of transgender people and that the party supports policies that support transgender people and upholds trans rights.
lobbying_activities: 382 emails were sent. - Email

records.specific_details: Re-launch All Party Group on Rare Diseases. Update on the need for a new National Strategy for Rare Diseases, including background data on rare diseases in Ireland and the challenges that people living with rare diseases face.
records.intended_results: Understanding of the rare disease landscape and the priorities of people living with rare diseases.
lobbying_activities: 1 hour meeting of Oireachtas All Party Group on Rare Diseases meeting to mark Rare Disease Day in the AV Room of Leinster House - Meeting
Invitation to attend Oireachtas All Party Group on Rare Diseases meeting to mark Rare Disease Day - Email

records.specific_details: To seek help for our not-for-profit Social Enterprise to allow us to bid for an existing contract with OPW via OGP so we can get through a financial crisis and also to get additional funding from Pobal to help us through this crisis.
records.intended_results: To allow Elsa to bid for work, where we are the incumbent, or otherwise.  If not allowed to bid, to delay the tendering process for 12 months to allow us to find replacement work and stay in business.  ::To have our project recognised as being in need of additional support.

records.intended_results: Awareness and support among elected representatives for the NAI call for investment in community neuro-rehabilitation teams in line with the specific commitment within the Programme for Government ::Awareness of the wider Programme for Government Commitments to Neurology and Neuro-Rehabilitation Services

records.specific_details: Encourage DPO's to attend Hustings event re Carer issues held by FCI in advance of the General Election
records.intended_results: To get as many officials to attend the event and listen to Carer issues/ agenda & Gain a commitment from political parties that they will prioritise supports for carers if they form part of the next Government
